verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase. It is myristoylated and palmitoylated at its N-terminus, anchoring it to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane. Its catalytic SH1, regulatory SH2, and SH3 domains all face the cytoplasm; there is no transmembrane domain and no extracellular-facing domain at any stage of its lifecycle. Pre-no checklist: no documented cancer/cell-death outer-leaflet ecto-pool; no tissue-restricted outer-leaflet display; not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented PM minority pool accessible from outside; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to outer leaflet; no cell-surface-directed therapeutic targets the LYN protein body extracellularly. pMHC is not a discriminating story. The inner-leaflet lipidation is the sole membrane anchor and keeps all functional domains cytoplasmic.
